next_best_action: Investigate subspace relay compatibility with the latest comm array firmware update. Check for known signal dropout issues during antenna switching on the port-side array. Verify relay module firmware v3.2 is the latest supported version.
remediation_steps: Verify subspace relay module firmware v3.2 is the latest supported version
Check for known signal dropout issues during antenna switching on the port-side array
Test relay reconnection behavior after the latest comm array firmware update on a reference module
If issue persists, escalate to Deep Space Communications for relay firmware patch review

next_best_action: Investigate whether flight console crash and navigation displaying old waypoints are related to the recent console swap migration. Verify that crew profile and instrument data were properly transferred to the new console.
remediation_steps: Verify console provisioning and data migration completed successfully
Clear flight console cache and reinitialize if crash persists
Check navigation profile migration status and force waypoint cache resync
Confirm instrument software versions match the supported standard flight image
If issues persist after cache clear and resync, re-provision flight software on the new console

next_best_action: Dispatch immediate on-site support to Command Briefing Room with a portable backup holographic display. Time-critical: $2.3M treaty delegation arriving in 25 minutes.
remediation_steps: Dispatch technician to Command Briefing Room immediately
Bring portable backup holographic display from engineering supply bay
Check display power coupling, data conduit connections, and input source settings
If main holographic display cannot be restored, set up portable unit before delegation arrives
After briefing, schedule full diagnostic of the main Command Briefing Room display

next_best_action: Contact crew member to gather specifics: which subsystems are inaccessible, what anomaly readouts appear, what module they are using, and when the issue started. Cannot triage effectively without more detail.
remediation_steps: Contact crew member to gather specific details about the issue
Determine which subsystems and instruments are affected
Check if the issue is isolated to this crew member or affecting others in their division
Once root cause is identified, route to the appropriate specialist team

next_best_action: Close — this is an automated cryo-sleep auto-reply to a maintenance notification, not a support request. No action required.
remediation_steps: No action required — cryo-sleep auto-reply detected, close signal

next_best_action: Initiate threat containment investigation. Review authentication logs for unauthorized access from unknown sector, check data archive audit trail for document access from the suspicious coordinates, and verify that credential rotation and biometric re-verification are in effect.
remediation_steps: Review authentication logs for the crew member's account over the past 48 hours
Check data archive audit trail for any unauthorized file access or downloads from unknown sector
Verify credential rotation was successful and biometric verification is active
If unauthorized access is confirmed, escalate to Threat Response Command and notify data protection officer
Brief crew member on interim security measures and monitor account for further anomalies

next_best_action: Check service credentials and RBAC role assignments on the primary data bank in telemetry-core-prod. Restore the Data Bank Contributor role if it was removed during the subsystem cleanup.
remediation_steps: Verify service credential token expiration for the telemetry pipeline identity
Check RBAC role assignments on the primary data bank in telemetry-core-prod
Restore Data Bank Contributor role assignment if missing
Re-run the telemetry pipeline 'prod-sensor-ingest' and verify successful execution
Investigate why the role assignment was removed during the subsystem cleanup to prevent recurrence

next_best_action: Investigate telemetry core connection pool exhaustion immediately. This is a mission-critical incident — 40% of telemetry requests are failing regardless of the submitter's priority classification.
remediation_steps: Investigate connection pool configuration on the primary telemetry core replica
Identify and terminate long-running queries or leaked connections in the telemetry data banks
Increase connection pool size or restart the primary telemetry core as immediate mitigation
Verify secondary replica health and confirm it is handling redirected telemetry traffic
Post-incident: conduct root cause analysis on telemetry core connection pool exhaustion

next_best_action: Escalate airlock access issue for Habitat Ring 7 — third report without resolution. Verify airlock permissions in the updated access management system post-migration and cross-reference with SIG-2834 if accessible.
remediation_steps: Check crew member's airlock permissions in the updated access management system
Verify Habitat Ring 7 access rights were properly migrated from the old system
If permissions appear correct, test the biometric scanner and airlock control panel
Escalate to Crew Identity & Airlock Control if biometric scan and permissions are both confirmed working
Follow up with crew member to confirm resolution

next_best_action: Initiate new crew member provisioning workflow for Alex Rivera (arrival stardate March 31). Coordinate console, instrument software, access, and division membership provisioning across divisions. Target readiness by March 28.
remediation_steps: Requisition standard flight console with sensor array from hardware procurement
Prepare standard software image with navigation, telemetry, and analysis instruments
Request analytics module workspace access (telemetry-ml-prod) from systems operations
Request data catalog read access from Telemetry & Data Core
Request Habitat Ring 3 airlock access from Crew Identity & Airlock Control
Add Alex Rivera to the analytics division comm channel and crew manifest
Schedule equipment handoff and station walkthrough for March 28

next_best_action: Investigate biometric scan failure at the identity core. Check biometric registration status and recent policy changes in the identity core. Crew member can continue with backup access code in the meantime.
remediation_steps: Verify biometric registration status for this crew member in the identity core
Check for recent access policy or biometric calibration changes that may affect scan recognition
Have crew member verify biometric scanner module is updated to the latest firmware
If issue persists, remove and re-register the biometric profile in the identity core
Confirm biometric scan works after re-registration

next_best_action: Investigate sensor display flickering as primary issue — likely hardware or data conduit fault. Route the flight software license question separately to Mission Software Operations.
remediation_steps: Test with a replacement data conduit cable
Test sensor display with a different source module to isolate whether it's the display or the console
If flickering persists with new cable and source, schedule sensor display replacement
Create a separate signal for the flight software license expiry and route to Mission Software Operations

next_best_action: Initiate classified data breach investigation immediately. This is a potential regulatory incident involving PII exposure of 1,200 crew records under Interstellar Data Protection protocols. Determine scope of external access, identify who created the sharing link, and assess notification obligations.
remediation_steps: Confirm the external sharing link has been fully revoked and verify no other data archives have similar permissions
Pull data archive audit logs to identify who created the public sharing link and when
Analyze audit logs for any external access to the archive between February 15 and today
Engage Command and Compliance to assess Interstellar Data Protection notification obligations
Document findings for the incident report and regulatory record
Conduct a broader data archive permissions audit across all archives containing classified crew records

next_best_action: Close — this is a thank-you message, not a support request. No action required.
remediation_steps: No action required — positive feedback, close signal

next_best_action: Investigate navigation telemetry latency regression on the deep-space relay path before next scheduled jump window. Check signal path to the navigation beacon, recent antenna or routing changes, and whether the latency is on the ship's side or the beacon side.
remediation_steps: Check navigation telemetry latency metrics on the signal path between the ship's comm array and the navigation beacon
Review recent antenna alignment, routing, or relay changes that may affect the navigation path
Compare current latency baseline against historical data to confirm the regression
If latency is on the ship's side, escalate to Deep Space Communications for immediate remediation before next jump window
If beacon-side, contact beacon operations and notify navigation crew of potential impact

next_best_action: Investigate SSO token invalidation after credential rotation on flight planning system. Likely the SSO session needs to be reissued after the credential change. Time-sensitive due to mission planning deadline.
remediation_steps: Verify the crew member's identity core session and auth token are refreshed after the credential change
Check flight planning SSO configuration — confirm whether SSO uses biometric or token-based authentication
Reissue the flight planning SSO token or have the crew member log in with explicit credentials as a workaround
If SSO token regeneration doesn't resolve it, check whether the credential rotation policy affected the flight planning secure channel configuration
Confirm flight planning access is restored and the crew member can complete the mission planning deadline

next_best_action: Contact reporter on Deck 5 (Robert Chen, robert.chen2@station.local, Operations Division) to gather actual symptom details. Signal as submitted has insufficient detail to triage. Beacon transcription is too garbled to act on.
remediation_steps: Contact Robert Chen (Deck 5 near galley, Operations Division) to clarify the issue in person or via comm channel
Determine which instrument or subsystem is failing
Gather specific anomaly readouts and reproduction steps
Once the actual issue is identified, re-triage and route to the correct specialist team

next_best_action: Dispatch support to the Command Briefing Room immediately. Admiral-level VIP signal — treat as one priority level higher. Investigate tactical display sharing policy blocking screen share for this crew member. Active briefing with allied delegation.
remediation_steps: Dispatch technician to the Command Briefing Room immediately
Check access policies and display sharing permissions applied to Admiral Martinez's account
Verify whether the 'Sharing is blocked by command policy' error is caused by a data protection policy, display admin policy, or access restriction
As immediate workaround: have another officer share the tactical display on his behalf, or use the briefing room console with a different credential
Once the briefing is unblocked, investigate and resolve the underlying policy conflict

next_best_action: Initiate crew departure deprovisioning workflow for Jessica Palmer (last duty shift March 21). Coordinate across Crew Identity, Spacecraft Systems Engineering, and Telemetry & Data Core for account disable, console wipe, file transfer, and comm archive. Note: sensitive compliance files require special handling.
remediation_steps: Schedule identity core account disable for 1800 hours on March 21
Prepare comm relay and remote access revocation to trigger simultaneously with account disable
Coordinate with Spacecraft Systems Engineering for console wipe of assigned workstation (asset tag ST-C-4892)
Transfer data archive and shared file ownership to David Liu (david.liu@station.local)
Remove from all crew comm channels and division manifests
Archive comm logs per 7-cycle retention policy
Ensure sensitive compliance investigation files are preserved in a secured data vault and access is revoked

next_best_action: Investigate network fabricator queue issue on Deck 3. Multiple crew members affected and jobs are queuing but not fabricating, which suggests a network or fabricator controller issue rather than a terminal problem.
remediation_steps: Identify the fabricator model and check whether it is network-attached or connected via fabrication controller
Check fabrication controller spooler service status and restart if necessary
Verify network connectivity between the fabricator and the fabrication controller or direct network path
If network-connected, check the fabricator's network address and interface status
Clear stuck fabrication queue and test with a fresh fabrication job

next_best_action: Redirect to Facilities — briefing pod booking and provisions are not flight support functions. Provide brief guidance on how to book pods via the scheduling system (add pod resource) and direct provisions questions to Station Facilities.
remediation_steps: Reply with instructions: in the scheduling system, create a new briefing and select 'Add a pod' to search for available briefing pods
Note that provisions requests should go to the Station Facilities team, not flight support
If the crew member cannot see pod resources in the scheduling system, verify their account has the pod finder permission and route to Mission Software Operations if needed

next_best_action: Renew or replace the security certificate on the external comm relay before it expires on March 20. This is a mission-facing relay — expiry will break all external comm integrations. Treat as P1 given 3-day window and the fact that the original provisioner has departed the crew.
remediation_steps: Identify the current certificate authority and certificate type (public CA, internal PKI, or automated provisioning)
Determine whether auto-renewal was configured or if the cert was manually provisioned
If auto-renewal exists, trigger a manual renewal cycle and verify the new cert is issued
If manual, generate a CSR, obtain a new certificate from the CA, and install it on the external comm relay
Verify the new certificate is correctly installed and all comm links work without encryption errors
Configure auto-renewal and monitoring alerts to prevent recurrence

next_best_action: Investigate console performance degradation. Symptoms (slow response, cooling fans at maximum, pop-ups about 'updating system components') are more consistent with a stuck firmware update or background compliance scan than a breach, especially since threat scanner found nothing. Check firmware update status and run diagnostics.
remediation_steps: Check firmware update history — verify whether recent updates completed or are stuck retrying
Review system diagnostics for high CPU/memory processes (likely firmware update or compliance scan)
If updates are stuck, run firmware update troubleshooter or manually reset the update components
Verify threat scanner definitions are current and run a quick diagnostic to confirm no intrusions
If the crew member interacted with a suspicious transmission, forward the transmission to threat-analysis@station.local and notify Threat Response Command as a precaution

next_best_action: Confirm this is a spoofed transmission and advise the crew member NOT to interact with any embedded links. Forward the original transmission to threat-analysis@station.local for analysis. No breach has occurred if the crew member hasn't interacted.
remediation_steps: Reply to the crew member immediately: this is a spoofed transmission — do NOT interact with any links or provide any credentials
Have the crew member forward the original transmission to threat-analysis@station.local
Check whether the transmission bypassed the ship's comm filtering and report to Threat Response Command if so
If the crew member already interacted with the link, escalate to a full security incident and initiate credential reset

next_best_action: Investigate analytics compute module prod-analytics-01 stuck at boot sequence after scheduled firmware patching reboot. Command briefing dashboard dependent on this module — 2-hour window before the 0900 briefing.
remediation_steps: Check boot diagnostics and serial console output for the stuck state on the compute module
Attempt to connect via serial console to bypass the stuck boot sequence
If serial console access is possible, check if a startup script or configuration policy is hanging
If the module is unrecoverable, redeploy from the latest snapshot or restore from backup
Verify the analytics dashboard is accessible once the compute module is restored
Post-incident: review the firmware patching procedure to prevent boot failures after scheduled updates
